title: [BUG] workbook.xlsx.readFile fails when auto filters are applied on a column
author: mlstoppa
body:
## 🐛 Bug Report

workbook.xlsx.readFile fails when auto filters are applied on a column.

Lib version: 4.2.1
node version: v14.17.3

## Steps To Reproduce

My goal is to read an XLSX file to export it as CSV. When the XLSX file has an auto filter on a column, set with a single value then the following code

```javascript
  const workbook = new Excel.Workbook();
  if (!await workbook.xlsx.readFile(convertParams.path)) {
    return {
      success: false,
      messagge: 'Error reading Excel file'
    }
  }
```
fails with error: 
```
Error: 500  ->  Unexpected xml node in parseClose: customFilters
```
When the XLSX file has more than one value set for a given auto filter then the error is as follow:
```
Error: 500  ->  Unexpected xml node in parseOpen: {"name":"filter","attributes":{"val": "set value"},"isSelfClosing":true}
```
## The expected behaviour:

All or only filtered rows should be loaded into the workbook.

## Possible solution (optional, but very helpful):

Adding some options to workbook readfile method:

```javascript
{
"options" : {
"customFilters": true/false
}
}
```
to control whether filtered rows should be load or omitted. 
Might this be an option?

Thank you

comments.author: MuneebZafar13
comments.body:
Do need solution to this as filtered rows still give following error while reading file.

`Unexpected xml node in parseOpen: {"name":"filter","attributes":{"val":"20"},"isSelfClosing":true}`

comments.author: namoscato
comments.body:
_Looping in @Siemienik, who has recently merged to `master`._

I'm getting the similar error (initially described in https://github.com/exceljs/exceljs/issues/1633) when streaming a file that was saved with applied Excel table filters.

For example:

```ts
const readStream = new Stream.PassThrough();
readStream.end(buffer);

const workbook = new ExcelJS.Workbook();
await workbook.xlsx.read(readStream);
```

results in:

```
Unexpected xml node in parseClose: filters
```

Are there any plans to support this?

comments.author: makobasuri
comments.body:
I fixed the problem on my end, and was constructing a testcase, to be able to make a pr.
When I tested the exceljs code without my fix, I expected a failiure but everything was fine...so apparently, the fix for this is allready in master but not on npm. 4.3.0 was released in august 2021, so there was plenty of time for someone else to implement the (probably better) fix.

However, if you're not comfortable using using unreleased code in production, you can check out my commit for a quick fix until exceljs releases a new version.

comments.author: namoscato
comments.body:
Thanks for the note, @makobasuri!

@Siemienik, it looks like you have recent commits to master. Are there plans to cut another release? Do you have any context on this filter issue?

comments.author: andyyapwl
andylacko
comments.body: @namoscato , what do you think? this blocks me from using this lib to read xlsx. Right now I have to read the file with SheetJS, which causes issues on different places. Can you check this? It would be helpful for me

comments.author: Siemienik
comments.body:
I will take a look, but first has a lot of work on a diffrent places. First I plan to release 4.4.0 soon (see #2352) and gain control over PRs and issues (now there is too many open issues and prs).

Just for reminder, my effort on this project is doing mostly voluntarily. If any issue/bug or feature is essential for you, it's possible to help me reach it out by buying my consulting services: exceljs@siemienik.com
